violations.text: Allergen statement was lacking. Provided during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-200 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

Parfaits made with yogurt, fruit preserves, and granola were marked with dates exceeding 7 days. PIC stated parfaits were made on Sunday, July 13, 2026. Dates were corrected during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

Cookies and parfaits packaged in the food establishment are lacking complete labels. Sub-ingredients and/or allergen information is lacking. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2025

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. Test strips on site expired July 1, 2022. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the counter mixer have an accumulation of food residue and other debris, especially around hardware. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025
The dumpster area has an accumulation of leaf litter and vegetation. The dumpster area must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2025

The insulation on a refrigeration line inside the walk in cooler is in poor repair, frayed with portions worn off. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2025
The fan covers in the walk in cooler have an accumulation of dust. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2024

In the self service case, yogurt & granola parfaits, and cups of orange packaged in the food establishment are lacking complete labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Yogurt & granola parfaits, and cups of orange juice must be placed behind the counter, or properly labeled. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2024

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of soil residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized, and bagged ice will be used until then. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was ( ) parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be ( ) ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

Egg sandwich, and potato & cheese & onion knish observed held at 118-128 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Items were reheated in oven. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024

Yogurt & granola cups packaged in the food establishment are lacking complete labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of soil residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized, and also repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023
The hand sink located in the front service area was not in good repair, with a bucket of stagnant water stored below it. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2023
The ice machine was not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/11/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the storage shelves in the front service area have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023
